raw_consumer_statement: I closed my American Express credit card in XXXX, XXXX, and subsequently cut up and disposed of the card as my husband and I were no longer flying to destinations. On XX/XX/XXXX, I received a notice from American Express that my credit card charge was overdue. I called American Express to inform them that this was a fraudulent charge and needed to be sent to the attention of their fraud department. Subsequently, I received notice from XXXX XXXX that I had an overdue account ( American Express ), so I filed a dispute with them. I called American Express again to find out what they needed to investigate this matter and personally spoke with their fraud department representative, but they offered no help. I looked up the charge myself and gathered the following information : around XX/XX/XXXX, someone used my American Express credit card number to make a purchase at XXXX ( which has a XXXX XXXX, CA, base ) in the amount of {$200.00} for an event titled " Loud Luxury ''. I once again called American Express with this information as they continued to send me statements for this charge, and my call was forwarded to the fraud department, so I inquired as to what more information they required. They responded with a letter dated XX/XX/XXXX, informing me I did not provide sufficient information for them to conduct a reasonable review of my claim. On XX/XX/XXXX, they sent me a letter to let me know the charges were now {$510.00} and they were transferring my account to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, for collection.
